text: fat is 41.6g. omega3 is where things start to fall off. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
carbs is 28.1g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for a seed, you want real nutrient concentration, not just tiny-food hype.
protein is 17.9g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 10% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 198% DV, calcium at 108% DV, zinc at 62% DV stand out most. that is major mineral support.

on the plus side. tiny food with serious nutrient density because seeds often overperform for their size. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters. usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths.

now the downsides. calorie density still stacks up fast because tiny foods can still overshoot fast. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.
B tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
